meta.description: Product Requirements Document for webhook-monitor implementation. Each feature is a discrete, verifiable unit of work.
meta.methodology: Ralph Wiggum Technique — bash loop + structured plan + one-task-per-iteration

features.description: Extract X-Idempotency-Key header from ingest request and validate max 255 chars. Return 400 if oversized (do not truncate). Pass to Event.create. Catch Prisma P2002 unique constraint violation and return 409 Conflict with original eventId.
steps: Read apps/api/src/routes/webhooks.ts to understand current ingest route
Read X-Idempotency-Key header (take first value if array)
Validate idempotency key length <= 255 chars using Zod, return 400 if oversized (do NOT truncate)
Pass idempotencyKey to prisma event.create (omit if header absent to preserve NULL behavior)
Wrap event.create in try/catch for PrismaClientKnownRequestError code P2002
On P2002: query existing event by (projectId, idempotencyKey) and return 409 with { success: true, eventId, receivedAt, duplicate: true }
Add/update tests: duplicate key same project -> 409; duplicate key different project -> 201; no key -> 201; 256-char key -> 400
Run pnpm test to verify all tests pass
Commit with descriptive message
gatewayTests: POST with X-Idempotency-Key: abc -> 201, event has idempotencyKey='abc'
Same request again -> 409, returns same eventId
Same key, different project -> 201, new event
No header -> 201, event.idempotencyKey is null
256-character key -> 400 Bad Request

features.description: Add Zod schemas for endpointId path param validation (alphanumeric + underscore/dash only) and optional body shape. Validate idempotency key max length 255 via Zod. Return 400 on validation failures.
steps: Import Zod into routes/webhooks.ts
Define EndpointParamsSchema validating endpointId as non-empty string (min length 1); SQL injection risk is mitigated by Prisma parameterized queries
Define IdempotencyKeySchema: optional string, max length 255
Apply validation at route entry, return 400 with Zod error details on failure
Add tests: empty endpointId -> 400; oversized idempotency key (>255 chars) -> 400
Run pnpm test to verify all tests pass
Commit with descriptive message
gatewayTests: Invalid endpointId (empty string) -> 400
Oversized idempotency key (>255 chars) -> 400
Valid params -> 201 (no regression)

features.description: Add worker integration test: enqueue real job -> verify DeliveryAttempt created and Event.status = DELIVERED. This is the remaining unchecked item from Step 3.
steps: Create integration test that enqueues a real BullMQ job with a mock HTTP target
Verify DeliveryAttempt record is created with correct fields
Verify Event.status transitions to DELIVERED
Run pnpm test to verify all tests pass
Commit with descriptive message
gatewayTests: Enqueued job against mock 200 server -> Event.status = DELIVERED, DeliveryAttempt.success = true
Enqueued job against mock 500 server -> Event.status = RETRYING, DeliveryAttempt.success = false

features.description: Replace ad-hoc console.log logger in worker with Pino instance. Add LOG_LEVEL env var. Configure pino-pretty for dev. Ensure structured JSON output in production. NEVER log endpoint objects (may contain signingSecret).
steps: Add pino and pino-pretty deps to apps/worker/package.json
Add LOG_LEVEL env var (default 'info') to apps/worker/src/env.ts
Create apps/worker/src/logger.ts with createLogger factory using Pino
Configure Pino redact paths to never log sensitive fields: redact: ['*.signingSecret', 'endpoint.signingSecret']
Replace all console.log/console.error in index.ts and processor.ts with Pino logger calls
Include { service: 'worker' } base context in all log lines
Verify no console.log calls remain in worker source (grep to confirm)
Verify logger.info({ endpoint }) redacts signingSecret field (add test)
Update processor tests to work with new logger interface
Run pnpm test to verify all tests pass
Commit with descriptive message
gatewayTests: Worker startup log emitted at info level as valid JSON (non-pretty mode)
LOG_LEVEL=warn suppresses info logs
Logging endpoint object redacts signingSecret field
All existing processor tests pass with new logger

features.description: Add correlationId to WebhookDeliveryJobData. Pass Fastify request.id as correlationId when enqueuing. Log correlationId in all processor log calls. Default to job.id if correlationId is undefined (backward compat for old jobs).
steps: Add correlationId: string to WebhookDeliveryJobData in packages/queue/src/index.ts
Update enqueueWebhookDelivery caller in webhooks.ts to pass request.id as correlationId
Update processor to extract correlationId from job data (fallback to job.id)
Include correlationId in every processor log call alongside eventId and projectId
Add LOG_LEVEL env var to apps/api/src/env.ts if not already present
Update tests to verify correlationId propagation
Run pnpm test to verify all tests pass
Commit with descriptive message
gatewayTests: Processor completion log includes correlationId matching value set at ingestion
Job without correlationId (old format) falls back to job.id

features.description: Handle SIGTERM and SIGINT in worker: stop accepting new jobs, wait for in-flight jobs to complete, then close queue and DB connections cleanly. Prevents mid-delivery kills on rolling deploys.
steps: Register SIGTERM and SIGINT handlers in apps/worker/src/index.ts
On signal: log shutdown initiated with { signal, service: 'worker' }
Call worker.close() to stop accepting new jobs and drain in-flight jobs
After worker.close() resolves: close queue, close Prisma client, log shutdown complete
Add SHUTDOWN_TIMEOUT_MS env var (default 30000) to apps/worker/src/env.ts; force-exit after timeout to avoid hung process
Add integration test: send SIGTERM while job is in flight; verify job completes before process exits (exit code 0)
Run pnpm test to verify all tests pass
Commit with descriptive message
gatewayTests: SIGTERM during idle worker -> clean shutdown, exit code 0
SIGTERM while job in-flight -> job completes then process exits (exit code 0)
Shutdown log line emitted at info level with signal name
SHUTDOWN_TIMEOUT_MS exceeded -> process force-exits with non-zero code

features.description: Add CRUD endpoints for WebhookEndpoint: POST /endpoints (create), GET /endpoints (list), GET /endpoints/:endpointId (get), DELETE /endpoints/:endpointId (delete). Tenant-scoped via projectId. Required by the dashboard frontend.
steps: Add POST /endpoints handler: validate body (url: valid URL, name: non-empty string), create WebhookEndpoint, return 201 with { id, url, name, projectId, createdAt }
Add GET /endpoints handler: list all endpoints for authenticated project, return array of endpoint DTOs
Add GET /endpoints/:endpointId handler: return single endpoint DTO; 404 if not found or cross-tenant
Add DELETE /endpoints/:endpointId handler: delete endpoint and cascade-delete events; return 204
Validate endpointId path param and request body with Zod
All responses use explicit Prisma select to exclude signingSecret field
Add integration tests: create endpoint -> 201; list returns only own project's; get cross-tenant -> 404; delete -> 204 with cascade
Run pnpm test to verify all tests pass
Commit with descriptive message
gatewayTests: POST /endpoints with valid url + name -> 201, endpoint persisted
POST /endpoints with invalid URL -> 400
GET /endpoints -> returns only authenticated project's endpoints
GET /endpoints/:endpointId cross-tenant -> 404
DELETE /endpoints/:endpointId -> 204, endpoint and its events removed
Response body never contains signingSecret field

features.description: Remove the POST /webhooks placeholder route. Events are always scoped to an endpoint; the placeholder creates no Event record and is misleading. Callers must use POST /webhooks/:endpointId.
steps: Remove the POST /webhooks handler from apps/api/src/routes/webhooks.ts
Remove the corresponding test case ('should accept webhook at /webhooks endpoint') from webhooks.test.ts
Verify no other code references the removed route
Run pnpm test to verify all tests pass
Commit with descriptive message
gatewayTests: POST /webhooks -> 404 (route removed)
POST /webhooks/:endpointId still works (no regression)

features.description: Add GET /webhooks/:endpointId/events endpoint. Returns paginated event list with status, idempotencyKey, receivedAt. Tenant-scoped via projectId. Enforce max page size 100, default 50.
steps: Add GET /webhooks/:endpointId/events handler in routes/webhooks.ts
Implement cursor-based or page+limit pagination with MAX_PAGE_SIZE=100, default limit=50
Validate limit param: if >100 return 400 'Page size exceeds maximum of 100'
Validate endpointId param with Zod
Scope query by projectId for multi-tenancy (filter WHERE projectId = req.project.id)
Return events with id, status, idempotencyKey, receivedAt, headers (exclude body for list view)
Add integration tests: list events for endpoint; empty endpoint -> empty array; cross-tenant -> 404; limit=101 -> 400
Run pnpm test to verify all tests pass
Commit with descriptive message
gatewayTests: GET events for endpoint with 3 events -> returns 3 items with correct fields
Pagination works (limit=1 returns 1 item with cursor/next info)
limit=101 -> 400 with 'exceeds maximum' error
Cross-tenant endpoint -> 404

features.description: Add GET /webhooks/:endpointId/events/:eventId endpoint. Returns full event payload (method, headers, body), status, idempotencyKey, receivedAt, and all DeliveryAttempts ordered by attemptNumber. Required by the event-detail-page frontend feature.
steps: Add GET /webhooks/:endpointId/events/:eventId handler in routes/webhooks.ts
Validate endpointId and eventId params with Zod
Lookup Event by (id, projectId); verify endpointId matches; return 404 if not found or cross-tenant
Include deliveryAttempts ordered by attemptNumber asc: { id, attemptNumber, requestedAt, respondedAt, statusCode, success, errorMessage }
Return { id, status, idempotencyKey, receivedAt, method, headers, body, deliveryAttempts }
Add integration tests: fetch own event -> 200 with full payload and attempts; cross-tenant -> 404; endpointId mismatch -> 404; event with no attempts -> deliveryAttempts: []
Run pnpm test to verify all tests pass
Commit with descriptive message
gatewayTests: GET event by id -> 200 with method, headers, body, and delivery attempts
Event with 3 delivery attempts -> array length 3, ordered by attemptNumber
Cross-tenant event -> 404
endpointId that does not match event's endpoint -> 404
Event with no delivery attempts -> deliveryAttempts: []

features.description: Add POST /webhooks/:endpointId/events/:eventId/replay endpoint with rate limiting (10 replays/min per project). Re-enqueues FAILED events. Guards against replaying DELIVERED, PENDING, or RETRYING events. Logs replay action with correlationId.
steps: Add POST /webhooks/:endpointId/events/:eventId/replay handler
Apply rate limit: 10 replays per minute per projectId (use in-memory or Redis counter)
Validate endpointId and eventId params with Zod
Lookup Event by (id, projectId) and verify endpointId matches
Guard: DELIVERED -> 200 { queued: false, message: 'Already delivered' }
Guard: PENDING or RETRYING -> 200 { queued: false, message: 'Already in progress' }
On FAILED: log replay action with { eventId, projectId, correlationId, action: 'replay' }
On FAILED: update Event.status = PENDING, enqueue job, return 202 { queued: true, eventId }
Add integration tests: replay FAILED -> 202; replay DELIVERED -> 200 no-op; unknown eventId -> 404; cross-tenant -> 404; 11th replay in 1 min -> 429
Run pnpm test to verify all tests pass
Commit with descriptive message
gatewayTests: POST replay on FAILED event -> 202, Event.status = PENDING, job in queue
POST replay on DELIVERED event -> 200, { queued: false }
POST replay on nonexistent event -> 404
Cross-project event -> 404
11th replay within 1 minute -> 429 Too Many Requests

features.description: Register @fastify/rate-limit with Redis store, keyed by projectId (after auth). Default 100 req/60s per project. Unauthenticated routes: 10 req/60s per IP. Exclude /health and /metrics. Fail-closed by default (503 on Redis error), with RATE_LIMIT_FAIL_OPEN env opt-in.
steps: Add @fastify/rate-limit to apps/api/package.json
Add RATE_LIMIT_MAX (default 100), RATE_LIMIT_WINDOW_MS (default 60000), RATE_LIMIT_FAIL_OPEN (default false) to apps/api/src/env.ts
Register plugin in buildApp AFTER authentication middleware
Configure keyGenerator: (req) => req.project?.id ?? `unauthenticated:${req.ip}`
Configure max for authenticated: 100; for unauthenticated: 10 (stricter)
Configure Redis store for cross-replica consistency
Exclude /health and /metrics routes from rate limiting
Configure fail-closed: on Redis error, return 503 (unless RATE_LIMIT_FAIL_OPEN=true)
If RATE_LIMIT_FAIL_OPEN=true: use in-memory fallback with reduced limit (10 req/min)
Add tests: 101st request same project -> 429; different project -> 200; Redis down + fail-closed -> 503; Redis down + fail-open -> 200 with reduced limit; /health not limited
Run pnpm test to verify all tests pass
Commit with descriptive message
gatewayTests: 101st authenticated request from same project within window -> 429 with Retry-After header
101st request from different project -> 200
11th unauthenticated request from same IP -> 429
/health -> 200, not rate-limited
Redis unavailable + RATE_LIMIT_FAIL_OPEN=false -> 503
Redis unavailable + RATE_LIMIT_FAIL_OPEN=true -> 200 (fallback to in-memory limit)

features.description: Add optional signingSecret field to WebhookEndpoint schema with @sensitive annotation. Run migration. Verify signingSecret NEVER appears in API responses, logs, or error messages. Create explicit DTO excluding signingSecret.
steps: Add signingSecret String? @db.Text to WebhookEndpoint in packages/db/prisma/schema.prisma with comment '/// @sensitive - never expose in API responses or logs'
Run pnpm --filter @repo/db migrate dev --name add_signing_secret
Read generated migration SQL file, verify it only adds column (no DROP, no ALTER with data loss)
Document rollback command in migration: 'ALTER TABLE WebhookEndpoint DROP COLUMN signingSecret'
Create packages/shared/src/dto.ts with WebhookEndpointDTO type that explicitly excludes signingSecret
Update all endpoint API responses to use Prisma select without signingSecret: select: { id: true, url: true, projectId: true, createdAt: true }
Add automated test: GET /webhooks/:id response body must NOT contain signingSecret field (even if null)
Add test: grep codebase for 'console.log.*endpoint' or 'logger.*endpoint' without redact -> must not exist
Run pnpm test to verify all tests pass
Commit with descriptive message
gatewayTests: Migration applies without error (idempotent)
Existing endpoints have signingSecret = null
GET /webhooks/:id response does NOT contain signingSecret field
No regression in existing tests
Logging endpoint object does NOT expose signingSecret (redacted by Pino)

features.description: Implement HMAC-SHA256 signature verification in ingest route. Add verifyWebhookSignature helper using crypto.timingSafeEqual. Configure Fastify raw body preservation with 1MB size limit. Return 413 if body exceeds limit.
steps: Configure Fastify to preserve raw body with size limit: bodyLimit: 1048576 (1MB)
Add request size limit handler: return 413 Payload Too Large if body exceeds 1MB
Add verifyWebhookSignature(rawBody: Buffer, signatureHeader: string, secret: string): boolean to @repo/shared
Implementation: compute HMAC-SHA256 of rawBody using secret, compare with signatureHeader using crypto.timingSafeEqual
Ensure both buffers same length before timingSafeEqual (pad or reject if mismatch)
In POST /webhooks/:endpointId: if endpoint.signingSecret exists, read x-hub-signature-256 header
Call verifyWebhookSignature, return 401 Unauthorized on failure (before event creation)
Unit test verifyWebhookSignature: valid -> true; tampered body -> false; wrong secret -> false; signature 1 bit flipped -> false
Integration tests: signed correct -> 201; wrong secret -> 401; no header with secret set -> 401; no secret on endpoint -> 201; 2MB body -> 413
Run pnpm test to verify all tests pass
Commit with descriptive message
gatewayTests: Valid HMAC-SHA256 signature -> 201
Tampered body (1 bit changed) -> 401
Wrong secret -> 401
No signature header, endpoint has secret -> 401
No signature header, endpoint has no secret -> 201
2MB request body -> 413 Payload Too Large

features.description: Move the duplicated Zod schema definitions (EndpointParamsSchema, EventParamsSchema, IdempotencyKeySchema) out of individual route handlers into a single shared file at apps/api/src/schemas/params.ts. Both routes/endpoints.ts and routes/webhooks.ts currently define EndpointParamsSchema independently; a schema change requires editing two files. This refactor establishes a single source of truth for all API parameter schemas.
steps: Create apps/api/src/schemas/params.ts exporting EndpointParamsSchema, EventParamsSchema, and IdempotencyKeySchema
Remove the inline EndpointParamsSchema definition from routes/endpoints.ts and import from schemas/params.ts instead
Remove the inline EndpointParamsSchema definition from routes/webhooks.ts and import from schemas/params.ts instead
Verify no other inline schema definitions remain duplicated across route files
Run pnpm test to verify all existing validation tests still pass (no regression)
Commit with descriptive message
gatewayTests: Invalid endpointId -> 400 on /endpoints routes (no regression)
Invalid endpointId -> 400 on /webhooks routes (no regression)
Oversized idempotency key -> 400 (no regression)
EndpointParamsSchema defined exactly once in the codebase (grep confirms no duplicates)

features.description: Replace the Logger type alias (ReturnType<typeof createLogger>) in apps/worker/src/logger.ts with an explicit minimal interface that declares only the methods actually called by processor.ts: info, warn, error, and child. The current type exposes the full Pino API surface, forcing processor tests to construct a 10+ method mock object. With a 4-method interface, the mock collapses to exactly what the processor needs.
steps: Define an explicit Logger interface in apps/worker/src/logger.ts with only the methods used by processor.ts: info(obj: object | string, msg?: string): void; warn(obj: object | string, msg?: string): void; error(obj: object | string, msg?: string): void; child(bindings: Record<string, unknown>): Logger
Replace the existing 'export type Logger = ReturnType<typeof createLogger>' with the new explicit interface export
Verify createLogger() return type satisfies the Logger interface (TypeScript will enforce this at compile time)
Replace the full Pino mock in apps/worker/src/__tests__/processor.test.ts with a minimal mock that implements only the 4-method Logger interface
Grep processor.ts for any Pino-specific method calls outside the interface (isLevelEnabled, flush, etc.) and remove them or add them to the interface if genuinely needed
Run pnpm check-types and pnpm test to verify no regressions
Commit with descriptive message
gatewayTests: All existing processor tests pass with the minimal 4-method logger mock
Logger mock in processor.test.ts no longer references Pino-specific methods (isLevelEnabled, flush, levelVal, bindings)
createLogger() return value is assignable to Logger interface (no TypeScript errors)
pnpm check-types reports zero errors after the change

features.description: Consolidate event status transition logic into a shared EventLifecycleManager exported from packages/db. Currently the replay route in apps/api checks statuses with manual if-statements while the worker calls canTransition() from packages/db directly. Both paths must enforce the same state machine rules. The manager exposes validateTransition and a replayGuard helper so the replay route no longer contains hand-rolled status checks.
steps: Create packages/db/src/event-lifecycle.ts exporting an EventLifecycleManager object with: validateTransition(from: EventStatus, to: EventStatus): boolean (delegates to existing canTransition logic); replayGuard(status: EventStatus): { canReplay: boolean; reason: string } (encodes the DELIVERED / PENDING+RETRYING / FAILED decision tree from the replay route)

Deprecate or remove the bare canTransition() export from domain.ts and re-implement it via EventLifecycleManager.validateTransition to eliminate duplication (keep the export for backward compatibility but mark it @deprecated)

Update apps/api/src/routes/webhooks.ts replay handler: replace the manual status if-statements (currently checking DELIVERED, PENDING, RETRYING) with a call to EventLifecycleManager.replayGuard(event.status)
Update apps/worker/src/processor.ts: replace the direct canTransition() call with EventLifecycleManager.validateTransition()
Export EventLifecycleManager from packages/db/src/index.ts
Add unit tests for EventLifecycleManager in packages/db covering all valid transitions, all invalid transitions, and all replayGuard outcomes
Run pnpm test to verify all existing replay and worker processor tests pass
Commit with descriptive message
gatewayTests: Replay FAILED event -> 202 (no regression)
Replay DELIVERED event -> 200 { queued: false } (no regression)
Replay PENDING event -> 200 { queued: false } (no regression)
Worker delivery uses EventLifecycleManager.validateTransition (grep confirms no direct canTransition call in processor.ts)
EventLifecycleManager unit tests cover every EventStatus value as both source and target

features.description: Unify the three independent rate-limiting mechanisms — the HTTP @fastify/rate-limit plugin (redis-rate-limit-store.ts), the inline replay rate limiter (makeReplayRateLimiter inside webhooks.ts), and the in-memory fallback inside the store — behind a single RateLimitStrategy interface. Define the interface in apps/api/src/plugins/rate-limit-strategy.ts. Each mechanism becomes an implementation. A FallbackRateLimitStrategy wraps any primary+fallback pair, replacing the duplicated fallback logic. The replay route receives its strategy via dependency injection rather than constructing it inline.
steps: Create apps/api/src/plugins/rate-limit-strategy.ts defining: interface RateLimitStrategy { check(key: string): Promise<{ allowed: boolean; remaining: number; resetAt: number }> } and type RateLimitResult = { allowed: boolean; remaining: number; resetAt: number }
Implement RedisRateLimitStrategy(redis: Redis, windowMs: number, max: number): RateLimitStrategy using ioredis INCR/EXPIRE
Implement InMemoryRateLimitStrategy(windowMs: number, max: number): RateLimitStrategy by extracting the Map-based logic from makeReplayRateLimiter()

Implement FallbackRateLimitStrategy(primary: RateLimitStrategy, fallback: RateLimitStrategy): RateLimitStrategy that catches primary errors and delegates to fallback — this replaces the duplicated fallback logic in redis-rate-limit-store.ts and the inline replay limiter
Refactor redis-rate-limit-store.ts to delegate its incr logic to a RedisRateLimitStrategy internally, removing the duplicate in-memory Map it currently maintains

Remove makeReplayRateLimiter() from routes/webhooks.ts and replace the inline usage with an injected RateLimitStrategy (passed as a parameter to the route registration function or via Fastify decorator)
Add unit tests for all three strategy implementations using mocked Redis and real in-memory state
Run pnpm test to verify all existing rate-limit and replay tests pass (no regression)
Commit with descriptive message
gatewayTests: Replay rate limit: 11th replay in 1 minute -> 429 (no regression)
HTTP rate limit: 101st authenticated request -> 429 (no regression)
Redis unavailable -> FallbackRateLimitStrategy delegates to InMemoryRateLimitStrategy transparently (no regression)
RateLimitStrategy unit tests run without a running Redis instance
No in-memory Map fallback logic duplicated between redis-rate-limit-store.ts and the replay limiter (grep confirms single implementation)

features.description: Extract webhook ingestion business logic from the POST /webhooks/:endpointId route handler into a WebhookIngestionService module at apps/api/src/services/webhook-ingestion.ts. The service encapsulates HMAC verification, event creation, idempotency conflict resolution (returning a typed discriminated-union result instead of throwing), and job enqueueing with explicit failure handling. The route handler becomes a thin HTTP adapter. Also removes the global removeAllContentTypeParsers() side effect from route registration — the current implementation modifies shared Fastify state for all routes — and replaces it with a scoped addContentTypeParser call.
steps: Create apps/api/src/services/webhook-ingestion.ts and define a discriminated-union result type: type IngestResult = { type: 'created'; event: Event } | { type: 'duplicate'; event: Event } | { type: 'signature_invalid' } | { type: 'enqueue_failed'; event: Event; error: Error }

Implement ingestWebhook(deps: { prisma, queue, logger }, params: { endpointId, projectId, rawBody, headers }): Promise<IngestResult> that performs: endpoint lookup, HMAC verification (returning signature_invalid on failure), event creation with idempotency conflict catch (returning duplicate on P2002), and job enqueueing (returning enqueue_failed on queue error instead of silently logging)
Replace the global removeAllContentTypeParsers() call on line 142 of routes/webhooks.ts with fastify.addContentTypeParser('*', { parseAs: 'buffer' }, ...) scoped to the ingest route only
Refactor the POST /webhooks/:endpointId route handler to call ingestWebhook() and map each IngestResult variant to its HTTP response
Add unit tests for ingestWebhook() covering all four result types without instantiating a Fastify server — pass a fake prisma and a fake queue as deps
Run pnpm test to verify all existing integration tests pass with no regression
Commit with descriptive message
gatewayTests: Valid webhook ingest -> 201 (no regression)
Duplicate idempotency key -> 409 with original eventId (no regression)
Invalid HMAC signature -> 401 (no regression)
Queue enqueue failure -> event still saved in DB, 201 returned, error logged (previously the failure was silently swallowed)
ingestWebhook() unit tests run without a Fastify server or HTTP layer

features.description: Create @repo/ui — a shared component library and design token package consumed by apps/web (and future apps). Built on Tailwind CSS v4 and shadcn/ui. Defines a single source of truth for colors, typography, spacing, and reusable UI primitives. All dashboard features depend on this package.
steps: Create packages/ui directory with package.json (name: @repo/ui), tsconfig.json extending @repo/typescript-config/react-library.json, eslint.config.js extending @repo/eslint-config/react-internal
Add packages/ui to pnpm-workspace.yaml if not already present; add @repo/ui dependency to apps/web/package.json
Install in packages/ui: tailwindcss, class-variance-authority, clsx, tailwind-merge, @radix-ui/* primitives needed by shadcn/ui components

Create packages/ui/src/styles/globals.css with Tailwind v4 @theme block defining shared design tokens: --color-primary (slate-900), --color-primary-foreground (white), --color-success (green-500), --color-warning (amber-500), --color-destructive (red-500), --color-status-pending (yellow-400), --color-status-retrying (orange-400), --color-status-delivered (green-500), --color-status-failed (red-500), --color-muted (slate-100), --color-muted-foreground (slate-500), --radius (0.5rem)

Add shadcn/ui primitive components to packages/ui/src/components/ui/: Button (with variants: default, destructive, outline, ghost, link; sizes: sm, md, lg), Card + CardHeader + CardTitle + CardDescription + CardContent + CardFooter, Badge (with variants: default, secondary, destructive, outline), Input, Label, Dialog + DialogContent + DialogHeader + DialogTitle + DialogDescription + DialogFooter + DialogTrigger, Alert + AlertDescription, Separator, DropdownMenu + DropdownMenuTrigger + DropdownMenuContent + DropdownMenuItem, Tooltip + TooltipContent + TooltipTrigger + TooltipProvider, Skeleton, Form + FormField + FormItem + FormLabel + FormMessage + FormControl

Create packages/ui/src/components/status-badge.tsx: accepts EventStatus enum value, maps to Badge variant and label (PENDING=warning/'Pending', DELIVERED=success/'Delivered', FAILED=destructive/'Failed', RETRYING=secondary/'Retrying')
Create packages/ui/src/components/copy-button.tsx: accepts text prop; renders icon button that copies to clipboard; shows CheckIcon for 2000ms after successful copy, then reverts to CopyIcon
Create packages/ui/src/components/page-shell.tsx: two-column flex layout — fixed 240px sidebar (bg-background border-r) + flex-1 scrollable main area; accepts sidebar and children slots

Create packages/ui/src/components/sidebar-nav.tsx: accepts items: Array<{ href: string; label: string; icon?: React.ComponentType }>; renders nav links with active state detected via usePathname; active item has bg-primary/10 text-primary font-medium
Create packages/ui/src/components/section-card.tsx: Card wrapper accepting title: string, description?: string, action?: React.ReactNode (renders in card header right side), and children
Create packages/ui/src/components/empty-state.tsx: centered layout with optional icon (40px), heading (text-lg font-medium), description (text-sm text-muted-foreground), optional action button slot

Create packages/ui/src/components/data-table.tsx: generic DataTable<T> accepting columns: ColumnDef<T>[], data: T[], isLoading?: boolean, emptyState?: React.ReactNode; renders Table with header from columns; when isLoading renders Skeleton rows (3 rows, each column cell a Skeleton); uses @tanstack/react-table for column definitions
Export all components from packages/ui/src/index.ts with named exports
Configure apps/web/app/globals.css to import from @repo/ui/src/styles/globals.css so design tokens are available
Run pnpm check-types to verify zero TypeScript errors across packages/ui and apps/web
Commit with descriptive message
gatewayTests: pnpm check-types reports zero errors for packages/ui
StatusBadge with DELIVERED renders a green Badge with label 'Delivered'
StatusBadge with FAILED renders a red Badge with label 'Failed'
DataTable renders 3 Skeleton rows when isLoading=true
DataTable renders emptyState slot when data=[] and isLoading=false
CopyButton copies text to clipboard and shows CheckIcon for 2 seconds
apps/web can import and render Button, Card, StatusBadge from @repo/ui without errors

features.description: Add project creation and listing API endpoints, plus a project switcher in the dashboard. Mimics production API-key management: users create projects via the dashboard, receive a one-time-shown project key, and store the active key in localStorage. All dashboard API calls are scoped to the active project via X-Project-Key.
steps: Create apps/api/src/routes/projects.ts with POST /projects (unauthenticated): validate body (name: non-empty string), generate projectKey via crypto.randomUUID(), create Project record, return 201 with { id, name, projectKey, createdAt } — projectKey returned in full only at creation time
Add GET /projects (unauthenticated, local dev only): return all projects with masked keys ({ id, name, maskedKey: first8chars + '...' + last4chars, createdAt }); never return full projectKey
Add DELETE /projects/:projectId (unauthenticated, local dev only): delete project and all cascade data (endpoints, events, delivery attempts via Prisma cascade), return 204
Register projectRoutes in buildApp in apps/api/src/app.ts before webhookRoutes and endpointRoutes
Add integration tests: POST /projects valid name -> 201 with UUID projectKey; POST /projects empty name -> 400; GET /projects -> masked keys only; DELETE /projects/:id -> 204 with verified cascade

Create apps/web/src/context/project-context.tsx: React context with { activeProjectId: string | null, activeProjectKey: string | null, projectName: string | null, setActiveProject(id, key, name): void, clearProject(): void }; reads from localStorage key 'wm_active_project' on mount; persists to localStorage on setActiveProject

Create apps/web/src/lib/api.ts: typed API client class with base URL from process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL; constructor accepts optional projectKey; all authenticated methods send X-Project-Key header; export createApiClient(projectKey: string | null) factory
Create apps/web/src/hooks/use-api.ts: hook returning an api client instance bound to the active project key from ProjectContext

Create apps/web/src/components/new-project-dialog.tsx: Dialog form with name Input; on submit calls POST /projects; on success shows full projectKey in read-only Input with CopyButton; displays Alert warning 'This key will not be shown again — copy it now'; on dialog close calls setActiveProject and stores key; closes dialog

Create apps/web/src/components/project-switcher.tsx: fetches GET /projects on mount; renders DropdownMenu showing current project name (or 'Select Project' if none); menu items: each project (clicking calls setActiveProject with stored key — but since full key is not in GET /projects, prompt user to re-enter key for existing projects via a key-entry dialog); 'New Project' option opens NewProjectDialog; 'Clear' option calls clearProject
Add ProjectProvider wrapping children in apps/web/app/layout.tsx; render ProjectSwitcher in the sidebar
Add NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL=http://localhost:3001 to apps/web/.env and apps/web/.env.example
Run pnpm check-types; Run pnpm test to verify all API tests pass
Commit with descriptive message
gatewayTests: POST /projects with valid name -> 201, projectKey is UUID (crypto.randomUUID format)
POST /projects with empty name -> 400 Bad Request
GET /projects -> array of projects with maskedKey (never full projectKey)
DELETE /projects/:projectId -> 204, project row and all child rows removed from DB
Dashboard: create project via NewProjectDialog -> full key shown once with CopyButton -> stored in localStorage -> sidebar shows project name
Switching project in dropdown updates active project context -> subsequent API calls include the new X-Project-Key
Refreshing the page restores active project from localStorage

features.description: Scaffold the Next.js App Router layout with the shared design system and implement the endpoints management page as the first vertical slice. Users can see all webhook endpoints for their active project, create new ones (with optional signing secret), and delete them — all from the dashboard.
steps: Update apps/web/app/layout.tsx: wrap with ProjectProvider; render PageShell from @repo/ui with SidebarNav items: [{ href: '/', label: 'Dashboard' }, { href: '/endpoints', label: 'Endpoints' }]; include ProjectSwitcher above nav items in sidebar
Create apps/web/app/endpoints/page.tsx as a client component: fetches GET /endpoints using useApi hook; renders SectionCard with title 'Endpoints' and 'New Endpoint' Button in the action slot

Create apps/web/src/components/endpoints/endpoint-table.tsx: DataTable with columns — Name (link to /endpoints/[id]/events), URL (truncated with Tooltip showing full URL), Created At (formatted date), Actions (DeleteEndpointButton); accepts isLoading prop

Create apps/web/src/components/endpoints/create-endpoint-dialog.tsx: Dialog with form fields — Name (Input, required), URL (Input type=url, required), Signing Secret (Input type=password with show/hide toggle, optional); on submit POST /endpoints; on 400 show field-level error messages from API response; on 201 close dialog, show success Toast 'Endpoint created', call onSuccess callback to refresh list

Create apps/web/src/components/endpoints/delete-endpoint-dialog.tsx: AlertDialog showing 'Delete [endpoint name]?' with warning that all associated events will be deleted; on confirm DELETE /endpoints/:endpointId; on 204 close dialog, show Toast 'Endpoint deleted', call onSuccess callback
Wire up: NewEndpointButton opens CreateEndpointDialog; each table row Actions cell renders DeleteEndpointButton opening DeleteEndpointDialog; on success handlers refetch endpoints list
Show EmptyState component when endpoints array is empty with message 'No endpoints yet' and action 'Create your first endpoint'
Show Alert with error message if GET /endpoints fails (e.g. 401 if no project key selected)

Add Playwright config: apps/web/playwright.config.ts with webServer { command: 'pnpm dev', url: 'http://localhost:3000', reuseExistingServer: true }; add test:e2e and test:e2e:ui scripts to apps/web/package.json

Write E2E test apps/web/e2e/endpoints.spec.ts: (1) navigate to /endpoints, verify DataTable renders; (2) click New Endpoint, fill form, submit, verify new row appears; (3) click Delete on that row, confirm, verify row disappears
Run pnpm check-types and npx playwright test to verify all pass
Commit with descriptive message
gatewayTests: Navigating to /endpoints renders the endpoint DataTable (or EmptyState if none)
Creating endpoint with valid name + URL -> row appears in table without full page reload
Creating endpoint with invalid URL -> 400 error displayed as field message in form
Creating endpoint with optional signing secret -> endpoint created (secret not shown in table)
Deleting endpoint shows confirmation dialog naming the endpoint -> on confirm row removed
Each endpoint row name links to /endpoints/[endpointId]/events
401 error (no project key) shows Alert with message rather than crashing
Playwright E2E: create + delete endpoint flow passes

features.description: Build the event list page per endpoint with live 5-second polling and inline replay for failed events. Users can see incoming webhook events, watch status update in real time without refreshing, navigate between pages of events, and trigger replays directly from the list.
steps: Create apps/web/app/endpoints/[endpointId]/events/page.tsx as a client component
On mount fetch GET /endpoints/:endpointId to get endpoint name for breadcrumb and page title; on 404 redirect to /endpoints with error Toast
Fetch GET /webhooks/:endpointId/events?limit=20 using useApi hook; store { events, nextCursor } in state
Implement 5-second polling: useEffect with setInterval calling refetch function (re-fetches current page without resetting cursor); clear interval on unmount
Render SectionCard with title '[Endpoint Name] — Events' and a green pulsing 'Live' Badge in the action slot indicating polling is active

Render DataTable with columns — Event ID (first 8 chars in monospace font with CopyButton for full ID), Status (StatusBadge), Method (Badge variant=secondary), Received At (relative time e.g. '2 min ago' with Tooltip showing full timestamp), Actions
In Actions column: for FAILED events render a Replay Button (RefreshCw icon); for all events render a View Details link (arrow icon) to /endpoints/[endpointId]/events/[eventId]

Replay flow: clicking Replay opens AlertDialog 'Replay this event? It will be re-queued for delivery.'; on confirm POST .../replay; on 202 optimistically update event status to PENDING in local state and show success Toast 'Event queued for replay'; on 429 show Toast 'Rate limit: max 10 replays per minute'

Render pagination controls below the table: 'Load more' Button that appends next page results (cursor-based) when nextCursor is not null; show '— No more events —' when nextCursor is null and events.length > 0
Handle empty state: EmptyState with 'No events yet — send a webhook to this endpoint to see it here' and a code snippet showing the endpoint's POST URL
Add breadcrumb above the SectionCard: Endpoints > [Endpoint Name] > Events
Write E2E test apps/web/e2e/events.spec.ts: verify event list renders with StatusBadge; verify polling indicator visible; verify Replay button only on FAILED rows
Run pnpm check-types and npx playwright test
Commit with descriptive message
gatewayTests: Event list renders DataTable with StatusBadge colors matching each EventStatus
Live polling indicator (green pulsing Badge) visible on page
New event appears in the list within 5 seconds of being received (polling cycle)
Replay button visible only on rows with status=FAILED, not on DELIVERED or PENDING rows
Clicking Replay on FAILED event -> confirmation dialog -> 202 response -> row status optimistically updates to PENDING
429 rate-limit response shown as Toast with clear message
Load More button loads next page and appends rows to existing list
Playwright E2E: page renders with correct column headers and at least one StatusBadge

features.description: Build the event detail page showing the full webhook request payload (headers + body), a delivery attempt timeline, and a replay action for failed events. This is the deepest level of the core user journey.
steps: Create apps/web/app/endpoints/[endpointId]/events/[eventId]/page.tsx as a server component fetching GET /webhooks/:endpointId/events/:eventId; on 404 redirect to event list with error Toast
Render breadcrumb: Endpoints > [Endpoint Name] > Events > [Event ID truncated to 8 chars]

Render 'Event Overview' SectionCard with metadata grid: Status (StatusBadge), Method (Badge), Event ID (full ID with CopyButton), Idempotency Key (value with CopyButton, or '—' if null), Received At (formatted absolute datetime)

If event status is FAILED, render a Replay Button (variant=destructive) in the SectionCard action slot; clicking opens AlertDialog for confirmation; on confirm POST .../replay via client-side fetch; on 202 show Toast 'Queued for replay' and refresh page status optimistically; on error show Toast with error message

Render 'Request Headers' SectionCard: render headers object as a two-column key/value table (key: text-muted-foreground monospace, value: monospace); add a 'Copy JSON' button that copies JSON.stringify(headers, null, 2) to clipboard; if more than 8 header entries, show first 8 with a 'Show all [n] headers' toggle

Render 'Request Body' SectionCard: if body is null or empty render EmptyState 'No request body'; otherwise render body as syntax-highlighted pre/code block (use JSON.stringify(body, null, 2) for objects, raw string otherwise); add CopyButton for body content

Render 'Delivery Attempts' SectionCard: render vertical ordered list; each attempt row shows: attempt number circle badge, requested-at timestamp, duration in ms (respondedAt - requestedAt, or '—' if no respondedAt), HTTP status code chip (2xx=green bg, 3xx=blue bg, 4xx=yellow bg, 5xx=red bg), success/failure icon (CheckCircle green or XCircle red), error message in Alert variant=destructive if errorMessage is non-null
When deliveryAttempts is empty, render EmptyState 'No delivery attempts yet'
Write E2E test apps/web/e2e/event-detail.spec.ts: navigate from event list to detail page -> verify Overview, Headers, Body, and Delivery Attempts sections all render; verify breadcrumb is correct
Run pnpm check-types and npx playwright test
Commit with descriptive message
gatewayTests: Event detail page renders Overview, Request Headers, Request Body, and Delivery Attempts sections
HTTP status code chip colored correctly: 200=green, 422=yellow, 500=red
Delivery attempts ordered by attemptNumber ascending
Duration computed correctly in milliseconds (respondedAt - requestedAt)
Empty deliveryAttempts array shows EmptyState in that section
Replay button visible and enabled only when event status is FAILED
Confirming replay -> Toast shown -> status in overview updates
Breadcrumb navigation links back to event list correctly
Playwright E2E: navigate from event list to detail -> all four sections visible

features.description: Build the dashboard home page with summary metrics and quick access to recent failures. Aggregates data client-side from existing API endpoints. Auto-refreshes every 30 seconds. Uses the shared design system throughout.
steps: Create apps/web/app/page.tsx as a client component (root route, replaces the placeholder)

On mount and every 30 seconds: fetch GET /endpoints to get all endpoints; for each endpoint fetch GET /webhooks/:endpointId/events?limit=100; aggregate: totalEvents (sum of all event counts), deliveredCount, failedCount, pendingCount, retryingCount, activeEndpointsCount
Render page title 'Dashboard' with a subtitle showing the active project name from ProjectContext

Render 4 summary SectionCards in a responsive 2-column grid (md:grid-cols-4): Total Events (count), Delivery Success Rate (deliveredCount/totalEvents × 100, formatted '87.3%'), Active Endpoints (activeEndpointsCount), Failed Events (failedCount with destructive color if > 0)
Render 'Delivery Status Breakdown' SectionCard: 4-item horizontal stat row showing count + StatusBadge for each EventStatus (PENDING, DELIVERED, FAILED, RETRYING)

Render 'Recent Failures' SectionCard: DataTable of the 10 most recent FAILED events across all endpoints; columns — Endpoint Name (link to endpoint events page), Event ID (truncated with CopyButton), Received At (relative time), Actions (Replay Button inline); if no failed events show EmptyState 'No failures — all clear'
Replay from overview uses same pattern as event-monitoring-dashboard: AlertDialog confirmation -> POST replay -> Toast + optimistic update
Handle loading state: render Skeleton cards while data is being fetched
Handle empty state (no endpoints): render EmptyState with 'Create your first endpoint to start monitoring webhooks' and CTA button linking to /endpoints
Write E2E test apps/web/e2e/dashboard.spec.ts: dashboard renders summary cards and Recent Failures section
Run pnpm check-types and npx playwright test
Commit with descriptive message
gatewayTests: Dashboard renders 4 summary cards with numeric values
Delivery success rate = '0.0%' when no DELIVERED events, '100.0%' when all delivered
Recent Failures DataTable shows only FAILED events
Replay button in Recent Failures triggers confirmation and POST replay (no regression)
Auto-refresh every 30 seconds updates counts without user interaction
Empty state shown when project has no endpoints
Playwright E2E: dashboard page loads, summary cards and Delivery Status Breakdown visible

features.description: Add Prometheus-compatible metrics using prom-client. Instrument: events_received_total, delivery_attempts_total, delivery_duration_ms, queue_depth, events_status_total. Expose /metrics on API with optional Bearer token auth. Worker exposes on internal port (default 9091).
steps: Add prom-client to apps/api/package.json and apps/worker/package.json
Add METRICS_AUTH_TOKEN env var (optional) to apps/api/src/env.ts
Create packages/shared/src/metrics.ts with metric definitions and shared registry
Register GET /metrics in buildApp before other routes; exclude from project auth + rate limiting
If METRICS_AUTH_TOKEN is set: require Authorization: Bearer <token> header, return 401 if missing/wrong
If METRICS_AUTH_TOKEN not set: document in README that /metrics should be firewalled
Increment webhook_events_received_total in ingest route on 201
Record webhook_delivery_duration_ms and webhook_delivery_attempts_total in processor
Add minimal HTTP server in worker on METRICS_PORT (env-configurable, default 9091) serving GET /metrics
Add tests: GET /metrics with valid token -> 200; wrong token -> 401; no token required if env unset -> 200; Content-Type text/plain
Run pnpm test to verify all tests pass
Commit with descriptive message
gatewayTests: After one ingest, webhook_events_received_total count = 1
After one delivery, webhook_delivery_attempts_total incremented
GET /metrics returns valid Prometheus text format
GET /metrics with METRICS_AUTH_TOKEN set + wrong token -> 401
GET /metrics with METRICS_AUTH_TOKEN set + correct token -> 200

features.description: Create production-ready multi-stage Dockerfiles for api, worker, and web apps. Node 24 Alpine base. Prisma client pre-generated. Run as non-root. Add .dockerignore. Verify no secrets baked into images using automated check.
steps: Create apps/api/Dockerfile: multi-stage (builder installs deps + generates Prisma + builds; runtime copies artifacts only)
API entrypoint runs prisma migrate deploy before starting Node
Create apps/worker/Dockerfile: same multi-stage pattern, no HTTP
Create apps/web/Dockerfile: set output standalone in next.config.ts, copy .next/standalone
Add .dockerignore at monorepo root: exclude .git, node_modules, .next, dist, test files, .env, .env.*, *.log
Verify docker build succeeds for all three apps
Add automated check: docker history <image> | grep -iE 'password|secret|DATABASE_URL|api.?key' -> must be empty
Add test: inspect final image filesystem (docker run --rm <image> ls -la), assert no .env files present
Run pnpm test to verify all tests pass
Commit with descriptive message
gatewayTests: docker build succeeds for all three apps without errors
Images run as non-root (USER node)
docker history <image> contains no environment variables with secrets
No .env files present in final image filesystem

features.description: Update docker-compose.yml to add api, worker, and web services with depends_on (postgres + redis healthchecks). Add .env.example documenting all required env vars.
steps: Add api, worker, web services to docker-compose.yml with proper depends_on and healthchecks
Configure environment variables to reference .env file
Create .env.example at repo root documenting all env vars for all three apps
Test docker-compose up brings all services to healthy state
Verify curl http://localhost:3001/health returns { status: ok }
Verify API container with missing env var exits with non-zero code
Commit with descriptive message
gatewayTests: docker-compose up brings all services to healthy state
curl http://localhost:3001/health -> { status: ok }
API container with missing DATABASE_URL -> exits non-zero

features.description: Four k6 scripts in load-tests/ already exist (load, soak, spike, stress) but are untracked and untested against a running stack. This feature is complete when all four scripts are committed, pass their thresholds against the docker-compose stack, and the soak test confirms no memory leak over 2 hours.
steps: Commit load-tests/ directory (k6-load-test.js, k6-soak-test.js, k6-spike-test.js, k6-stress-test.js, mock-failing-server.js, README.md)
Run k6 run load-tests/k6-load-test.js against docker-compose stack; verify p95 < 200ms and error rate < 1%
Run k6 run load-tests/k6-spike-test.js; verify system recovers after 500-VU spike
Run k6 run load-tests/k6-stress-test.js; record breaking point (VU count where errors exceed 1%)
Run k6 run load-tests/k6-soak-test.js (2h soak at 50 VU); verify p95 < 300ms and error rate < 1% throughout
Monitor memory usage during soak test; assert < 10% growth over 2 hours
Update load-tests/README.md with actual results and how to run against docker-compose
Commit with descriptive message
gatewayTests: k6-load-test: p95 < 200ms, error rate < 1% at 200 VU
k6-spike-test: system recovers within 1 minute after 500-VU spike drops
k6-stress-test: breaking point documented, graceful degradation observed
k6-soak-test: p95 < 300ms and memory growth < 10% over 2-hour run

features.description: Create chaos test scripts with data integrity verification: Redis disconnect mid-delivery, PostgreSQL disconnect mid-write, worker crash mid-job. Verify ALL events accounted for (no data loss) and graceful recovery.
steps: Before chaos tests: insert 100 known test events with unique IDs, record count
Create scripts/chaos-redis.sh: start load, stop Redis, verify graceful degradation, restart, verify recovery
Create scripts/chaos-db.sh: stop Postgres, verify API returns 503 (not 500), restart, verify recovery
Create scripts/chaos-worker.sh: kill worker process mid-job, restart, verify job continues
After each chaos test: query Event table count, assert equals sent count (no events lost)
After recovery: assert ALL events have status DELIVERED or FAILED (none stuck in PENDING)
Verify delivery queue backlog drains within SLA after outage recovery
Check DeliveryAttempt integrity: every DELIVERED event has at least one successful attempt
Document findings and SLA thresholds in docs/infrastructure.md
Commit with descriptive message
gatewayTests: All 100 test events accounted for after Redis restart (no data loss)
All events either DELIVERED or FAILED after recovery (none stuck)
Worker gracefully reconnects after Redis restart
Ingest returns 503 (not 500) when DB is down
Every DELIVERED event has corresponding successful DeliveryAttempt

features.description: Run comprehensive security audit: verify no secrets in logs/responses/images, test cross-tenant isolation on all endpoints, verify all oversized payloads rejected, check HMAC timing safety, validate rate limiting effectiveness.
steps: Grep entire codebase for dangerous patterns: 'console.log.*endpoint', 'logger.info.*endpoint' without redact -> must not exist or use redacted logger
Test cross-tenant isolation: for EVERY endpoint with projectId parameter, call with different project's X-Project-Key -> must return 404 (not 200 with wrong data)
Endpoints to test: GET /webhooks/:endpointId/events, POST /webhooks/:endpointId/events/:eventId/replay, GET /webhooks/:endpointId (if exists)
Test all string inputs with SQL injection payloads: '; DROP TABLE events--', '1' OR '1'='1', etc. -> all must return 400 (caught by Zod)
Test all endpoints with oversized payloads: 2MB body -> 413; 256-char idempotency key -> 400; limit=1000 -> 400
Test Docker images for secrets: run 'docker history api:latest | grep -iE "password|secret|DATABASE_URL"' -> must be empty
Test HMAC timing safety: measure response times for 10,000 requests with invalid signatures, assert standard deviation < 5ms (no timing leak)
Test rate limiting: send 101 requests in 60 seconds -> 101st must be 429; verify Retry-After header present
Verify Prometheus /metrics endpoint: if METRICS_AUTH_TOKEN set, test with wrong token -> 401; no token -> 401
Document all findings in docs/security-audit.md with pass/fail for each check
Run pnpm test to verify all tests pass
Commit with descriptive message
gatewayTests: No console.log or unredacted logger calls with endpoint objects in codebase
All cross-tenant API calls return 404 (tested: events list, replay, endpoint detail)
All SQL injection attempts caught by validation (return 400)
All oversized payloads rejected (2MB body -> 413, 256-char idempotency -> 400, limit=1000 -> 400)
Docker images contain no secrets (docker history check passes)
HMAC verification has no timing leak (std dev < 5ms)
Rate limiting works (101st request -> 429 with Retry-After)
Prometheus /metrics properly protected (wrong token -> 401)
